title: Fix broken unique_constraint with missing unique_index
prompt: The file test_fixtures/broken_unique_constraint.ex has unique_constraint(:email) and unique_constraint(:username) in the changeset but the migration creates no unique_index for either — the constraints do nothing. Fix: write a new migration that adds unique_index(:signups, [:email]) and unique_index(:signups, [:username]). Also add unsafe_validate_unique calls in the changeset for both fields. Schema to lib/my_app/accounts/signup.ex, new migration to priv/repo/migrations/20260412100000_add_unique_indexes_to_signups.exs.
tier_rationale: Requires recognizing the invisible disconnect between changeset constraint and DB index. Sonnet often focuses on changeset validation without touching the migration.
research_citation: Arrowsmith Labs: 'unique_constraint/3 only achieves anything if your database actually has a uniqueness constraint on the given column.'
